_comment: Kling scene library v2 (youth-viral, 2026-07-10) — 10 Gen-Z scenes from a judged multi-agent creative pass. Branding (hook text, logo pill, end card) is added IN POST by video_edit.brand_full_bleed, so scenes keep top/bottom of frame calm and never render text/logos. video_gen.py rotates one scene per day (date ordinal) and fills {who}/{idea}/{hook}.

scenes.template: One continuous handheld front-camera UGC shot, 5 seconds, no cuts. A {who}, 16-24, attractive and expressive, lying in bed in a near-black bedroom at 3am, face filling the middle of frame, lit only by cold violet-purple light rising from a phone held just below the frame edge — {idea}. Opening frame: extreme close-up, eyes wide with dread, lips parted, breath held, the violet under-glow flickering across the cheekbones like a message just landed; pure black headboard shadow above the face, dark duvet below the chin. Beat 1, 0-2s: they stare down toward the unseen phone, swallow, jaw tightening, the phone light trembling — pure overthinking panic. Beat 2, 2-3s: one quick decisive thumb motion just below the frame edge — a single tap — and a bright violet-purple pulse washes upward over their whole face like the room exhaled. Beat 3, 3-5s: no smirk, just done — a small arm motion drops the phone face-down onto the duvet below frame, the glow snuffs out to faint ember, their eyes roll shut mid-breath and they are instantly, comically asleep, face slack with total peace. Mood: {hook}. 24mm phone-selfie look, slight handheld micro-shake, single violet key light, deep blacks, shallow ambient grain, the phone screen never visible — only its glow.

One continuous 5-second shot, no cuts. Camera: 35mm lens, shallow depth of field, one slow push-in from medium close-up to close-up with subtle organic micro-shake, like a front phone camera propped against a coffee mug — raw UGC texture. Setting: a cramped dorm desk at 1am, warm tungsten desk lamp as the only room light, a blurred leaning tower of textbooks, crumpled paper balls, a half-crushed energy drink can, {idea} scattered across the desk in soft bokeh. Subject: {who}, a real-looking student aged 16-24, hoodie sleeves pulled over hands, cheek squished onto one fist, one eye half-closed, face split by two light sources — warm amber lamp glow on one side, cold violet-purple phone glow flickering up from below the frame edge on the other. Opening frame: that exhausted split-lit face dead center, total academic burnout. The phone buzzes — the violet glow strobes once against their chin and they give a dead-eyed thousand-yard stare, too fried to form words. Then one quick off-hand thumb flick just below the bottom of frame — a single bright violet pulse washes up their face like a soft wave. Payoff: shoulders drop, head tips back for half a beat — then they faceplant gently forward into the open textbook, one arm dangling, out cold, the violet glow settling to a slow calm heartbeat beside them. Energy: {hook}. Moody low-key amber-versus-violet contrast, gentle film grain.

One continuous 5-second shot, horror-movie-turned-epic treatment of a mundane late-night message. A pitch-black teenage bedroom at 2am, lit only by a smartphone lying face-up on rumpled bedding, radiating an otherworldly abstract violet-purple column of light toward the ceiling like an ancient artifact just awakened — volumetric god-rays, dust motes drifting in the beam, faint neon spill from a window. Opening frame: the glowing relic-phone center frame, and at the edge of its light {who} half-lit in violet, blanket pulled to the chin, eyes wide with theatrical dread, because {idea}. Camera: one slow low-angle push-in across the bed toward phone and face, 24mm lens, deep blacks, A24-horror color grade. At the 3-second mark a hand enters as a clean dark silhouette against the beam and makes one quick confident downward thumb motion at the phone's edge — and the entire column of light blooms into a single soft satisfying pulse that washes the room like a wave of calm, then collapses to a gentle ember heartbeat. Their dread dissolves: a slow exhale, and they flop backward onto the pillow, blinking up at the ceiling with a flat, unbothered, done-with-it expression — the relic appeased, {hook} energy, the room finally quiet. Pure black ceiling above, dark bedding below, the screen only ever abstract violet light.

One continuous 5-second shot, no cuts. Camera: 0.5x ultra-wide lens with mild fisheye edge distortion, a single smooth backward dolly glide retreating down a library aisle at walking pace — the whole shot is one move. Setting: a moody university library at night, a narrow canyon of tall dark-wood shelves, rows of warm green-gold banker's lamps glowing in deep bokeh, dust motes in the beams, {idea} in the scene details. Opening frame: {who}, a stylish student aged 16-24, layered fit and backpack hanging off one shoulder, half-crouched between the shelves like they are hiding from the message itself, phone held low near their hip mostly out of view, face underlit in cold violet-purple glow, silently mouthing different phrasings and rejecting every one — the universal second-language how-do-I-even-say-this freeze, jaw tight, eyes darting. Action: one fast, decisive thumb flick low at the hip, quick and blurred, no finger close-up — then the violet glow pulses once, bright, splashing purple light up the shelves on both sides like a silent shockwave. Their whole body unclenches: they straighten, give one tiny shrug, let the phone arm swing loose out of frame, and stroll toward the retreating camera completely unbothered, almost bored, {hook} energy, drifting out of the violet afterglow into the warm lamplight. Dark academia meets neon: deep shadows, golden practicals, one violet accent, dark ceiling shadow above, dark carpet below.

One continuous 0.5x ultra-wide shot, 5 seconds, a single slow push-in as the only camera move, no cuts. A {who}, 16-24, styled in a going-out fit, alone in a neon-soaked party bathroom — teal and pink LED glow smeared on wet tiles, faint haze, lights softly throbbing like bass through the wall — hunched over the sink, head bowed over a phone held low against the counter that throws hard violet-purple light up onto their face: {idea}. Opening frame: white-knuckle grip on the sink edge, face tight with drafting agony, the violet under-light stuttering in irregular flickers like a reply being typed and deleted, typed and deleted, the ultra-wide lens bending the tiled walls around them. Beat 1, 0-2s: the camera pushes in slowly as they chew their lip, eyes darting, a full overthinker spiral over what to send. Beat 2, 2-3s: one fast flick of the thumb low near the sink, mostly out of view — a single tap — and the whole bathroom answers: a clean violet pulse sweeps across the tiles and their face, briefly outshining the neon, the flickering finally steady. Beat 3, 3-5s: they simply stand up straight, take one slow breath, let a small half-smile land, and turn toward the door. Energy: {hook}. Crushed blacks, glossy neon highlights, club-grade color, dark ceiling haze above, dark sink shadow below, the phone only an abstract violet light source.

One continuous 5-second shot filmed like real front-camera UGC, handheld with natural sway, no cuts. Setting: the staircase of a dim house party at night — warm string lights tangled along the banister, bodies and colored light blurred far in the background haze, muffled bass implied by soft throbbing glow on the walls. Opening frame: {who} sitting on a step in the middle of frame holding a phone low at their lap, its violet-purple glow uplighting their worried face, with two friends leaning in from either side one step above — three faces clearly separated, not touching, all locked onto the glow with absurd emergency-summit seriousness, because {idea}. The mood is {hook}. Beat 1, 0-2s: silent deliberation — one friend shakes their head slowly, the other covers their mouth, the phone owner breathes like a general before an order. Beat 2, at 3s: the owner makes one swift decisive thumb motion low at their lap, tap kept small and quick — instantly the violet glow flares in a single bright pulse up the stairwell walls. Payoff, 3-5s: the two friends detonate — grabbing each other's arms, silently screaming, one falling back against the banister laughing — while the owner stays perfectly still in the middle of the chaos and gives one slow, deadpan nod. Warm tungsten and string-light bokeh versus one violet accent, natural skin texture, dark upper wall above, dark step shadow below, screen never shown — glow only.

One continuous handheld UGC-style shot on a 24mm lens, 5 seconds, one slow push-in, filmed like a friend is holding the camera, no cuts. Setting: a dark college dorm room at night, violet-purple LED strips glowing softly along the wall, everything else in shadow. Opening frame: {who} sits cross-legged on the bed dead center, face tense in the violet under-glow of a phone held just below the frame line — {idea} — while one best friend hangs over their shoulder from behind, chin practically on their collarbone, gesturing wildly with both hands, clearly dictating the perfect reply word by word with total confidence. Energy: {hook}. Beat 1, 0-2s: the friend keeps coaching, dramatic hand flourishes, while the phone holder's eyes glaze over — they are not listening. Beat 2, at 3s: mid-flourish, the holder makes one quick decisive thumb tap below the frame line — a bright violet pulse washes up across both faces. Beat 3, 3-5s: the friend freezes mid-gesture, jaw dropping in pure betrayed disbelief — then cracks into delighted laughter and shoves the holder's shoulder, while the holder just tosses the phone gently aside onto the duvet and shrugs, already over it. Two faces only, clearly separated, big legible expressions. Cinematic low-light look, soft film grain, natural skin against violet glow, dark empty wall above, dark bedding below, the screen never legible — abstract glow and soft pulses only.

One continuous cinematic shot, 5 seconds, a single slow lateral dolly as the only camera move, no cuts. A {who}, 16-24, hoodie up, earbuds in, alone in a window seat on a nearly empty night bus, profile filling the middle of frame against rain-streaked glass, city lights streaking past as soft orange-and-blue anamorphic bokeh — {idea}. A violet-purple glow from a phone held low below frame stutters on their face in irregular bursts, like a reply being typed, deleted, retyped. Opening frame: eyes down, brow knotted, breath fogging a small circle on the cold glass, a faint ghost of their reflection in the wet window — the loneliest drafting session of their life. Beat 1, 0-2s: the camera drifts slowly sideways; the stuttering glow gets faster, they inhale sharply and sit up — the agonizing becoming unbearable. Beat 2, 2-3s: one small decisive thumb motion below frame — a single tap — and a clean bright violet flash blooms up across their face and the wet glass like a signal sent into the night. Beat 3, 3-5s: all the tension drains — they exhale, huff a small laugh at themselves, and let their head thunk gently against the window, eyes following the passing lights, the violet glow settling warm and steady. Mood: {hook}. Deep blacks, soft film grain, rainy-night palette with one violet accent, dark bus ceiling above, dark seat shadow below, no visible screen — glow and reflections only.

One continuous 5-second shot, no cuts. Camera: 35mm lens, one slow lateral dolly gliding along an empty back row of a large daytime lecture hall toward the subject. Setting: pale cold daylight through high windows, a projector beam cutting hazy light across the room, rows of empty seats in soft bokeh, a distant blurred lecturer far below — and {who}, 16-24, slouched alone in the very back row in a hoodie, hiding a phone low beneath the fold-down desk, its violet-purple glow leaking up onto their chin and cheeks against the flat classroom daylight — {idea}. Opening frame: that guilty violet under-glow on a face performing fake attention, eyes flicking between the front of the hall and their lap, lips pressed tight, mid-crisis in the middle of class. Beat 1, 0-2s: they sneak one long look down, panic flashing across their face, then dart their eyes back up to the front like a hunted animal. Beat 2, at 3s: one tiny, decisive thumb flick beneath the desk, hidden and quick — a soft violet pulse blooms up from under the desk across their face. Beat 3, 3-5s: they slide slowly back in the seat, cross their arms, fix their eyes on the distant board, and bite down on a grin they absolutely cannot suppress. Energy: {hook}. Naturalistic daylight grade with one violet accent, fine grain, dark ceiling shadow above the frame, dark seat-backs below, no readable slides or writing anywhere — the projector shows only abstract soft light.

One continuous 5-second shot, no cuts. Camera: 0.5x ultra-wide front-camera look with mild fisheye edges, a single smooth backward glide retreating ahead of the subject at walking pace. Setting: a city sidewalk at night, wet pavement reflecting smeared neon storefront colors — pinks, teals, ambers — light haze, a couple of blurred strangers far behind. Opening frame: {who}, 16-24, in a sharp streetwear fit, mid-stride toward the camera with full main-character energy, headphones around neck, neon sliding across their face — then they STOP dead mid-step, frozen center frame, as a violet-purple glow flares from a phone held low at their waist just below clear view: {idea}. Beat 1, 0-2s: the freeze — eyes drop, face falls into an oh-no stare, neon swirling around their stillness, the violet under-glow trembling. Beat 2, at 3s: one fast decisive thumb flick low at the waist, quick and blurred — a bright violet pulse washes up their body and flares off the wet pavement around their sneakers. Beat 3, 3-5s: they bark one short laugh, shake their head, and resume the strut right past the retreating camera without breaking stride again, {hook} energy, swallowed back into the neon. Glossy night-street color, crushed blacks, one violet accent against the multicolor neon, dark sky and awnings above the frame, dark wet pavement below, no signage ever legible — storefronts are pure abstract color blur.

style_suffix: Vertical 9:16 composition, 5-second single continuous take, no cuts, one camera move maximum. Absolutely no on-screen text, letters, numbers, captions, signage or logos anywhere in frame; any phone screen appears only as abstract violet-purple glow, soft glowing card shapes and light pulses, never readable UI. Keep the top 15% of frame and the bottom 10% of frame calm, dark and empty — no faces, hands or key action there; all money-shot action lives in the middle 70% of frame. Cinematic low-light contrast, natural realistic skin texture, subtle film grain, TikTok-native authenticity.
negative_prompt: garbled text, gibberish letters, words, captions, subtitles, signage, watermark, logo, warped fingers, extra fingers, fused hands, deformed hands, warped face, melted face, face morphing, extra limbs, distorted phone, bent phone, visible phone UI, readable screen, low quality, blurry, oversharpened, jpeg artifacts
